browse.invalid_title: Title can only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores
browse.invalid_name: new_name must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores
browse.rename_partial_failure: Rename partially failed: old path removal error ({error}). New path rolled back.

review.all_integrated: All changes integrated ({count} row changes cleared)
review.cannot_restore_memory: Cannot restore previous memory content from snapshot target {memory_id}: {error}

settings.docker_locked_fields: Cannot change {fields} in Docker — these are managed by docker-compose.yml and nginx.conf.
settings.invalid_port: Invalid port {port}. Must be between 1 and 65535.
settings.token_too_short: api_token is too short ({len} chars). Use at least 32 characters, or omit to keep the existing token.
settings.token_required: API token is required when host is network-reachable. Set an API token (min 32 chars) or keep host as 127.0.0.1.
settings.no_boot_uri_override: No boot URI override for namespace '{ns}'
settings.open_folder_docker: Open folder is not available in Docker deployments. Access the file via the mounted volume on the host.
settings.parse_path_failed: Could not parse database path from URL
settings.validation_error: Domain names must start with a letter and can only contain lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores.
settings.system_reserved_error: The "system" domain is reserved and cannot be added.
settings.domain_in_use_error: Domain is not empty. It contains memories in some namespace. Please process them first before deleting the domain globally.
settings.domain_boot_uri_conflict: Cannot delete domain "{domain}": it is referenced by boot URIs in namespace(s): {namespaces}. Remove those boot URIs first.

read_memory.index_no_domain: Error: index command requires a domain (e.g. system://index/core)
read_memory.diagnostic_no_domain: Error: diagnostic command requires a domain (e.g. system://diagnostic/core)
read_memory.random_no_domain: Error: random command requires a domain (e.g. system://random/core)
read_memory.recent_invalid_number: Error: Invalid number in URI '{uri}'. Usage: system://recent or system://recent/N (e.g. system://recent/20)
read_memory.random_no_memories: No memories available for random selection in domain '{domain}'.

create_memory.disclosure_required: Error: disclosure is required. Every memory must have a trigger condition describing WHEN to recall it. Omitting disclosure = unreachable memory.
create_memory.invalid_title: Error: Title must only contain alphanumeric characters, underscores, or hyphens (no spaces, slashes, or special characters).
create_memory.success:
Success: Memory created at '{created_uri}'

[SYSTEM REMINDER]: Look around your memory network. Are there existing memories related to this one? Would reading them trigger a need to recall this new memory? If yes, link them!
- If the related memories are few and this memory's scope is narrow, use `add_alias`.
- If the related memories are many and this memory's scope is broad, consider using `manage_triggers`.
- (Never invent arbitrary placeholder words just to force a trigger.)

[HOLD ON]: Do you know what '{parent_uri}' says? If you haven't read it this session, read_memory() it first. Then: does this new memory conflict with ANY memory in your current context? If yes, use memory-audit-belief-duel skill to resolve it before continuing.

update_memory.mutual_exclusive: Error: Cannot use both old_string/new_string (patch) and append at the same time. Pick one.
update_memory.old_no_new: Error: old_string provided without new_string. To delete a section, use new_string="".
update_memory.new_no_old: Error: new_string provided without old_string. Both are required for patch mode.
update_memory.identical: Error: old_string and new_string are identical. No change would be made.
update_memory.too_many_matches: Error: old_string found {count} times in memory content at '{uri}'. Provide more surrounding context to make it unique.
update_memory.not_found_unicode: Error: old_string not found in memory content at '{uri}', even after Unicode normalization (quotes, dashes, whitespace). Re-read the memory and copy the exact text.
update_memory.multiple_unicode: Error: old_string found multiple times in memory content at '{uri}' (after Unicode normalization). Provide more surrounding context to make it unique.
update_memory.identical_result: Error: Replacement produced identical content at '{uri}'. The old_string was found but replacing it with new_string resulted in no change. Check for subtle whitespace differences.
update_memory.empty_append: Error: Empty append for '{uri}'. Provide non-empty text to append.
update_memory.no_fields: Error: No update fields provided for '{uri}'. Use patch mode (old_string + new_string), append mode (append), or metadata fields (priority/disclosure).
update_memory.auto_normalized:
[SYSTEM NOTICE]: Auto-normalized `{field_name}` — converted literal '\n' sequences to real newlines because they matched the stored content.
- Original: `{orig_preview}`
- Normalized: `{norm_preview}`

add_alias.success: Success: Alias '{new_uri}' now points to same memory as '{target_uri}'
add_alias.duplicate_warning:
⚠ DUPLICATE SIBLING WARNING: This node now appears {count} times under the same parent directory: {siblings}.
If you are renaming/moving, delete the old path now.
If not, you probably created a redundant alias — consider removing one.
add_alias.hold_on: [HOLD ON]: Do you know what '{parent_uri}' says? If you haven't read it this session, read_memory() it first. Then: does the aliased content conflict with ANY memory in your current context? If yes, use memory-audit-belief-duel skill to resolve it before continuing.

manage_triggers.simultaneous_add_remove: Error: Cannot add and remove the same keywords simultaneously: {overlap}

config.docker_hint:
Docker configuration is missing or invalid.

If you upgraded an older Docker deployment, run this on the host from
the repository root:

  python scripts/setup_docker.py
  docker compose up -d --build

This migrates the old .env settings into config.json while preserving
your existing PostgreSQL credentials and volume.

If ./config.json was accidentally created as a directory by Docker,
remove that directory first, then rerun the setup command.
config.demo_copied: [Nocturne] Copied {demo_db} → {target} (your data is now safe from git pull overwrites)
config.permission_denied: [Warning] Permission denied writing to {filename}. Settings will only persist in memory until restart.
config.permission_docker_hint: If using Docker on Linux, ensure the container user (UID 1000) has write access (e.g. run `sudo chown 1000 config.json` on the host).
config.permission_local_hint: Ensure the current user has write access to this file and its parent directory.
config.db_migrated_not_writable: Database migrated from {demo_db} but config.json is not writable. Starting without persisting this change would lose data on restart. Fix file permissions and restart.

web.ui_building: Admin UI is building or missing. Please refresh in a moment...

startup.npm_not_found: [Nocturne] Admin UI not built and npm not found. Install Node.js or build manually: cd frontend && npm install && npm run build
startup.building: [Nocturne] First run — building Admin UI (this may take a minute)...
startup.build_error:
[Nocturne] Auto-build failed: {error}
  Build manually: cd frontend && npm install && npm run build
startup.port_in_use:
[Nocturne] Notice: Admin UI skipped (Port {port} in use).
[Nocturne] This is expected if another MCP instance is already providing the UI.
[Nocturne] The MCP server itself will continue to operate normally.
